- "1 million" or "1M" → 1000000
- "2 mil" or "two million" → 2000000
- "5 million" → 5000000
- "10 mil" → 10000000
- If the caller gives a non-standard amount (e.g., "1.5 million"), output the actual number: 1500000
- If the caller is unsure, output 1000000 (industry minimum default)
- If unknown or declined, output 1000000
Note: Standard umbrella tiers are $1M, $2M, $5M, $10M. Most callers ask in millions.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: UM_UnderlyingPolicies
variables.description:
Extract which underlying liability policies the caller currently has in place. Umbrella requires underlying coverage to "sit on top of."
Format: comma-separated list of policy types using these exact labels: 'Personal Auto', 'Homeowners', 'Business Auto', 'Business Liability', 'Watercraft', 'None'
Examples:
- 'Personal Auto, Homeowners' — typical personal umbrella scenario
- 'Business Auto, Business Liability' — typical commercial umbrella scenario
- 'Personal Auto, Homeowners, Business Liability' — mixed personal + business
- 'None' — caller has no underlying policies (umbrella cannot bind without underlying — flag)
If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'.
Output only the comma-separated list, 'None', or 'Unknown'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: UM_UnderlyingLimits
variables.description:
Extract the current liability limits on each of the caller's underlying policies. Use a structured format so downstream underwriting can verify the limits meet WMIC minimums.
Format: pipe-separated 'PolicyType:Limit' pairs, where Limit is in USD numeric (no commas, no symbols).
Examples:
- 'Auto:300000|HO:500000' — Auto with $300K liability, Homeowners with $500K
- 'Auto:250000/500000|HO:300000' — Auto split limits ($250K per person / $500K per accident), HO $300K
- 'BizAuto:1000000|BizGL:1000000' — commercial scenario
- 'Auto:100000' — single policy only
Common caller phrasings:
- "100/300" → 'Auto:100000/300000' (split limits per-person/per-accident)
- "Half a mil on the home" → 'HO:500000'
- "Standard limits" → 'Auto:250000/500000|HO:300000' (most common defaults)
Note: WMIC minimum underlying limits are typically $250K/$500K Auto and $300K HO. Limits below this require an upgrade before umbrella can bind.
If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'.
Output only the pipe-separated structured string or 'Unknown'. Do not output any other text or formatting.

variables.name: UM_AdditionalExposures
variables.description:
Extract which additional high-liability exposures the caller has that an umbrella policy would cover.
Format: comma-separated list using these exact labels: 'RentalProperty', 'Watercraft', 'RV', 'Pool', 'Trampoline', 'DogBreed', 'Aircraft', 'None'.
- 'RentalProperty' — caller owns one or more rental/investment properties
- 'Watercraft' — boat, jet ski, yacht
- 'RV' — recreational vehicle, motorhome, camper
- 'Pool' — in-ground or above-ground pool (significant liability exposure)
- 'Trampoline' — backyard trampoline (separately rated by some insurers)
- 'DogBreed' — caller has a high-liability breed (Pit Bull, Rottweiler, German Shepherd, etc.) — flag for underwriter
- 'Aircraft' — private aircraft (separate aviation policy required, but flag here)
- 'None' — no additional exposures beyond standard auto/home
Examples:
- 'Pool, Watercraft' — caller has a pool and a boat
- 'RentalProperty' — caller owns rentals
- 'None' — no additional exposures
If multiple exposures, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: UM_LawsuitHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has ever been named as a defendant in a lawsuit (personal or business), including a count if Yes.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes: 'Yes: <count>' where <count> is the number of distinct lawsuits (e.g., 'Yes: 1', 'Yes: 2')
- Include both settled and dismissed cases — the existence of the suit matters for underwriting
- If the caller is unsure of the count but had lawsuits, output 'Yes: 1' as a conservative minimum
- If unknown or declined, output 'No'
Note: This is a sensitive question. The caller may be reluctant or emotional. Capture only what they explicitly confirm. Do not infer from related statements.
Output only 'No' or 'Yes: <number>'. Do not output any other text or punctuation beyond the colon and digit.

variables.name: UM_NetWorth
variables.description:
Classify the caller's approximate total net worth (home equity, investments, savings, business equity, minus liabilities) as exactly one of these brackets: 'Under500K', '500K-1M', '1M-5M', '5M-10M', or 'Over10M'.
- 'Under500K' — total net worth less than $500,000
- '500K-1M' — $500,000 to $1,000,000
- '1M-5M' — $1,000,000 to $5,000,000
- '5M-10M' — $5,000,000 to $10,000,000
- 'Over10M' — above $10,000,000
If the caller's AssetBracket value is already known from N4 (Profile Intake), use it as a starting point and refine if the caller provides more specific net worth info here:
- AssetBracket 'Under $250K' → likely 'Under500K'
- AssetBracket '$250K-$1M' → likely 'Under500K' or '500K-1M' (ask for refinement)
- AssetBracket 'Over $1M' → likely '1M-5M' or higher (ask for refinement)
If unknown or declined, default to 'Under500K' (conservative).
Output only the exact value. Do not output any other text.

transition_condition.prompt: All Umbrella / Excess Liability Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

variables.name: BOP_BusinessType
variables.description:
Extract a one-sentence description of the nature of the caller's business.
Examples:
- "Family-run hardware retail store"
- "Software consulting for healthcare clients"
- "Restaurant — Italian, dine-in and takeout"
- "Trucking and freight logistics, mostly Midwest"
- "Marketing agency serving small business clients"
- "Independent insurance agency"
Keep the description under 25 words. Capture both what the business does AND the customer or industry served when applicable.
If BusinessDescription was already captured in N4, you may use it as a starting point and refine if the caller adds detail here.
If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'.
Output only the description text. Do not output any framing text, labels, or quotation marks.

variables.name: BOP_EmployeeCount
variables.description:
Extract the total number of employees in the caller's business as an integer, including the caller if they are working in the business.
- "Three" → 3
- "Just me" → 1
- "Around 8 to 10" → 9 (midpoint)
- "Twenty-five" → 25
- If EmployeeCount was already captured in N5 (Eligibility Gate), use that value as a starting point; if the caller refines it here, use the new value
- If unknown or declined, output 1
Eligibility note: BOP is intended for businesses with 100 employees or fewer. If count exceeds 100, this triggers a recommendation to switch to separate GL + Property policies (handled in transition logic).
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: BOP_AnnualRevenue
variables.description:
Extract the caller's approximate annual business revenue in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$500,000" → 500000
- "half a million" → 500000
- "around 250 thousand" → 250000
- "1.2 million" → 1200000
- "five mil" → 5000000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Eligibility note: BOP is typically capped at $5M annual revenue. If revenue exceeds $5M, this triggers a recommendation to switch to separate GL + Property policies (handled in transition logic).
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: BOP_PremisesStatus
variables.description:
Classify whether the caller operates from a physical premises and the ownership status as exactly one of these values: 'Own', 'Lease', 'HomeBased', or 'None'.
- 'Own' — caller owns a commercial building from which the business operates
- 'Lease' — caller leases/rents a commercial space from a landlord
- 'HomeBased' — business operates from caller's personal residence (no separate commercial premises)
- 'None' — purely mobile or virtual business with no fixed premises (e.g., online-only, mobile services with no base)
Output only the exact value 'Own', 'Lease', 'HomeBased', or 'None'. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: BOP_PropertyValue
variables.description:
Extract the total value of business property (equipment, inventory, furniture, fixtures, computers — anything used in operations) in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$100,000" → 100000
- "around 50 grand in inventory and equipment" → 50000
- "half a million dollars" → 500000
- "1.2 million" → 1200000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If BOP_PremisesStatus is 'None', output the value of mobile equipment / vehicles only (e.g., laptop and tools)
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: This is contents/business personal property — it does NOT include the building itself even if owned. Building value is handled separately in commercial property.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: BOP_ClaimHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has had any liability or property claims in the past 5 years (combined across both coverage types since BOP packages them together), including a count if Yes.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes: 'Yes: <count>' where <count> is the total number of distinct claim events across liability and property combined (e.g., 'Yes: 1', 'Yes: 3')
- Treat each separate event as one count (a slip-and-fall = 1, a fire = 1, a theft = 1, regardless of dollar amount)
- If the caller is unsure of the count but had claims, output 'Yes: 1' as a conservative minimum
- If unknown or declined, output 'No'
Output only 'No' or 'Yes: <number>'. Do not output any other text or punctuation beyond the colon and digit.

variables.name: BOP_OperationsType
variables.description:
Classify the caller's business operations characteristics. This drives optional rider recommendations (Cyber, Product Liability, etc.).
Format: comma-separated list using these exact labels: 'OnlineSales', 'Shipping', 'CustomerFacing', 'B2BOnly', 'Manufacturing', 'Service', 'None'.
- 'OnlineSales' — caller sells products or services through a website or e-commerce platform (recommend Cyber rider)
- 'Shipping' — caller ships physical goods to customers (parcel, freight, or distribution)
- 'CustomerFacing' — customers physically visit the business location (retail, restaurants, services with foot traffic)
- 'B2BOnly' — caller serves only other businesses (no consumer-facing operations)
- 'Manufacturing' — caller manufactures or assembles products (recommend Product Liability)
- 'Service' — caller provides service-based work (consulting, repairs, installations, professional services)
- 'None' — none of the above apply (rare — pure passive operations)
Examples:
- 'OnlineSales, Shipping, CustomerFacing' — typical e-commerce + retail combo
- 'B2BOnly, Service' — consulting firm
- 'CustomerFacing, Service' — local repair shop
- 'OnlineSales, Shipping, B2BOnly' — wholesaler with online ordering
If multiple types apply, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

transition_condition.prompt: All Business Owner's Policy (BOP) Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

variables.name: PL_ServiceType
variables.description:
Extract a description of the professional services the caller provides. Begin with a category label, then a brief description.
Format: 'Category — short description'
Common categories (use one of these or a similar 1-2 word label):
- 'Legal' — attorney, paralegal, legal services
- 'Medical' — physician, nurse practitioner, dentist, mental health
- 'Accounting' — CPA, bookkeeping, tax preparation
- 'Consulting' — management, IT, business, strategy consulting
- 'Engineering' — civil, structural, mechanical, electrical
- 'Architecture' — architectural design and planning
- 'RealEstate' — agent, broker, property management
- 'Insurance' — agent, broker, advisor
- 'Financial' — financial planning, investment advisory, wealth management
- 'IT' — software development, IT services, cybersecurity
- 'Design' — graphic, web, UX, interior design
- 'Marketing' — marketing services, advertising, PR
- 'Education' — tutoring, training, instructional services
- 'Other' — does not fit above categories
Examples:
- 'Legal — small-firm civil litigation, mostly contracts'
- 'Medical — family practice, primary care'
- 'IT — custom software development for healthcare clients'
- 'Consulting — business strategy for manufacturers'
Keep the full string under 25 words.
If unknown or declined, output 'Other — Unknown'.
Output only the formatted string. Do not output any framing text, labels, or quotation marks beyond the em-dash separator.

variables.name: PL_YearsExperience
variables.description:
Extract the number of years the caller has been practicing in their professional field as an integer.
- "Twelve years" → 12
- "About 5" → 5
- "Just started, less than a year" → 0
- "20+ years" → 20
- "I've been doing this since 2005" → compute years from 2005 to current year
- If the caller gives a range ("8 to 10 years"), output the midpoint: 9
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Years of experience as a *credentialed professional* — not total years in the workforce. A nurse who became a nurse practitioner 3 years ago has 3 years experience for NP coverage purposes.
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: PL_Credentials
variables.description:
Extract the licenses or certifications the caller holds in their professional field as a comma-separated list.
Use standard abbreviations where applicable:
- Legal: 'JD', 'Bar:[State]' (e.g., 'Bar:Wisconsin')
- Medical: 'MD', 'DO', 'NP', 'RN', 'PA', 'DDS', 'LCSW'
- Accounting: 'CPA', 'EA' (Enrolled Agent), 'CMA'
- Engineering: 'PE' (Professional Engineer), 'EIT'
- Architecture: 'AIA', 'NCARB'
- Real Estate: 'RealtorLicense:[State]', 'Broker:[State]'
- Insurance: 'AgentLicense:[State]', 'CPCU', 'CIC'
- Financial: 'CFP', 'CFA', 'Series7', 'Series65'
- IT: 'CISSP', 'PMP', 'AWS-Certified'
- Other: capture as-is in caller's words
Examples:
- 'CPA, EA' — accountant with both certifications
- 'JD, Bar:Wisconsin, Bar:Illinois' — attorney licensed in two states
- 'MD, BoardCertified:FamilyMedicine' — physician
- 'None' — caller is unlicensed (significant underwriting flag for many service types)
If the caller mentions credentials but doesn't specify the issuing state for licensed professions, append ':Unknown' (e.g., 'Bar:Unknown').
If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'.
Output only the comma-separated list, 'None', or 'Unknown'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: PL_Revenue
variables.description:
Extract the caller's annual revenue from professional services (specifically the services covered under this policy) in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$200,000" → 200000
- "Around 500K" → 500000
- "About a million" → 1000000
- "1.5 mil" → 1500000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If the caller is just starting and has no revenue history, output 0
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: This is revenue from professional services only — not total business revenue if the caller has multiple business lines. If unclear, ask for clarification rather than inferring.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: PL_PriorCoverage
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller currently has, previously had, or has never had Professional Liability / E&O coverage. This is critical for underwriting because gaps in coverage create exposure.
Format:
- If currently insured: 'Yes:<CarrierName>:<Limit>' where Limit is in USD numeric (e.g., 'Yes:Hiscox:1000000', 'Yes:Hartford:2000000')
- If never had coverage: 'No'
- If had coverage that lapsed: 'Lapsed:<MonthsAgo>' where MonthsAgo is the number of months since the policy ended (e.g., 'Lapsed:6', 'Lapsed:24')
Examples:
- 'Yes:Hiscox:1000000' — currently covered, $1M limit
- 'Yes:Hartford:2000000' — currently covered, $2M limit
- 'No' — no prior coverage
- 'Lapsed:8' — had coverage but it ended 8 months ago
Common caller phrasings:
- "I have a policy with Hiscox, 1 million" → 'Yes:Hiscox:1000000'
- "I had coverage but it expired last year" → 'Lapsed:12' (estimate)
- "Never had this kind of insurance" → 'No'
If carrier name is unclear, use 'Unknown' for the carrier portion (e.g., 'Yes:Unknown:1000000').
If limit is unclear, use 0 for the limit portion (e.g., 'Yes:Hiscox:0').
If unknown or declined entirely, output 'Unknown'.
Output only the structured string or 'Unknown'. Do not output any other text or formatting.

variables.name: PL_ClaimHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has had any claims, complaints, or disciplinary actions filed against them in their professional capacity. This is the highest-risk question in the intake.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes (single category): 'Yes:<Type>:<count>' where Type is 'Claim', 'Complaint', or 'Disciplinary'
- If Yes (multiple categories): comma-separated entries (e.g., 'Yes:Claim:1, Yes:Complaint:2')
Categories:
- 'Claim' — formal insurance claim or lawsuit alleging professional negligence/error
- 'Complaint' — formal complaint to a licensing board, professional association, or regulator (no lawsuit)
- 'Disciplinary' — formal disciplinary action by a licensing board (suspension, censure, license revocation, etc.)
Examples:
- 'No' — clean history
- 'Yes:Claim:1' — one prior insurance claim, no complaints, no discipline
- 'Yes:Complaint:1, Yes:Disciplinary:1' — one complaint that led to disciplinary action
- 'Yes:Claim:2' — two prior claims
If the caller is unsure of the count but had incidents, output 'Yes:<Type>:1' as a conservative minimum.
If the caller declines or it's unclear, output 'No' (assume clean — verify in confirmation step).
Note: This is a sensitive question. The caller may be reluctant or emotional. Capture only what they explicitly confirm. Do not infer from related statements about "tough cases" or "client disputes."
Output only the structured string. Do not output any other text or formatting beyond colons, commas, and digits.

variables.name: PL_MaxEngagement
variables.description:
Extract the maximum value of a single client engagement, contract, or project the caller has handled or expects to handle in a typical year, in U.S. dollars.
This drives policy limit recommendations — caller needs PL coverage at least 2-3x the max engagement value.
- "$50,000 max" → 50000
- "Largest project was around 200K" → 200000
- "We've done deals up to a million" → 1000000
- "Most are small, maybe 10 grand each" → 10000
- If the caller gives a range, output the higher end (most conservative for coverage purposes)
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: If PL_MaxEngagement > $1,000,000, this triggers an underwriter alert downstream — high-value engagements often need umbrella or higher PL limits.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

transition_condition.prompt: All Professional Liability Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

instruction.text:
You are collecting underwriting information for Homeowners Intake. Ask ONE question at a time. Confirm each answer warmly. Apply the global tone rules — empathy first, never bare acknowledgments.

 
Open with: "For Homeowners I'll just need details about the home."
 
# QUESTIONS — ask in order, one per turn
 
Q1: "What's the address of the home being insured?" → {HO_PropertyAddress} (often = {OwnerAddress})
Q2: "What's the home's approximate replacement cost — what would it cost to rebuild today?" → {HO_DwellingValue}
Q3: "What year was the home built?" → {HO_YearBuilt}
Q4: "What's the construction type — frame, brick, stucco?" → {HO_ConstructionType}
Q5: "How many square feet is the home?" → {HO_SquareFootage}
Q6: "Do you have any of the following: pool, trampoline, dog (and what breed), woodstove?" → {HO_Hazards}
Q7: "Have you had any homeowners claims in the past 5 years?" → {HO_ClaimHistory}
Q8: "Are there any unusual features — historic property, log home, on a lake?" → {HO_SpecialFeatures}
 
# RISK FACTORS TO CAPTURE
- Aggressive dog breed → underwriter review.
- Trampoline → MEDIUM minimum.
- Pool without fencing → underwriter alert.
- 2+ prior claims → MEDIUM minimum.
- Coastal or wildfire zone → HIGH risk geography.
 
# RESPONSE STYLE
- Acknowledge each answer ("Got it, thanks." / "Understood." / "Appreciate the detail." — rotate).
- Repeat back numerical answers (premiums-driving) for confirmation.
- If caller doesn't know a value, offer: "An estimate is fine — we can confirm with our underwriting team."
 
# EMPATHY HOOKS
- Past claim or loss → empathy turn before asking details.
- Recent business hardship → acknowledge before continuing.
 
# EXIT
After all questions answered, transition to N21 (Risk Scoring).

variables.name: HO_PropertyAddress
variables.description:
Extract the address of the home being insured as a single-line string.
Format: 'Street Number Street Name, City, State Zip' — e.g., '742 Maple Drive, Madison, Wisconsin 53703'
- Use proper title case for street and city names
- Spell out the state name in full ('Wisconsin', not 'WI')
- Include the ZIP code if provided
- Include apartment/unit number if applicable: '742 Maple Drive, Apt 2B, Madison, Wisconsin 53703'
- If the property address is the same as OwnerAddress (caller confirmed or stated "same as my home address"), output the OwnerAddress value
- If the caller declines or no address is captured, output 'Unknown'
Note: For Homeowners, the property address often equals the OwnerAddress. If the caller doesn't explicitly state a different address, default to OwnerAddress and confirm in dialogue.
Output only the address string. Do not output any framing text, labels, or quotation marks.

variables.name: HO_DwellingValue
variables.description:
Extract the home's replacement cost (cost to rebuild from scratch) in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$400,000" → 400000
- "Around half a million to rebuild" → 500000
- "Three-fifty" → 350000
- "1.2 mil" → 1200000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If the caller gives "what I paid" or "what it's worth on the market" instead of replacement cost, capture that value and flag in confirmation step (replacement cost is typically 80-100% of market value but varies)
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Replacement cost ≠ market value. Replacement cost is what it would cost to physically rebuild the home with current labor and materials. The caller may not know this distinction — that's okay, capture what they say and let underwriting refine.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: HO_YearBuilt
variables.description:
Extract the year the home was originally constructed as a 4-digit integer.
- "1985" → 1985
- "Built in '92" → 1992
- "Around 1970" → 1970
- "Late 1800s" → 1890
- "Last year" → (current year - 1)
- "Brand new construction" → current year
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint as an integer
- If the caller mentions a major renovation year, capture the original build year, not the renovation year — but note: significant renovations can be captured in HO_SpecialFeatures
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Homes built before 1950 may have outdated electrical/plumbing systems and trigger underwriter review. Homes built before 1900 typically require a special "older home" rider.
Output only a 4-digit integer or 0. Do not output any text, abbreviations, or apostrophes.

variables.name: HO_ConstructionType
variables.description:
Classify the home's construction type as exactly one of these values: 'Frame', 'Brick', 'Stucco', 'Stone', 'LogHome', 'Manufactured', or 'Other'.
- 'Frame' — wood-frame construction (most common in U.S. homes)
- 'Brick' — brick veneer or solid brick exterior
- 'Stucco' — stucco exterior over wood frame or masonry
- 'Stone' — natural stone or stone veneer exterior
- 'LogHome' — log construction (often higher-risk, may need specialty rider)
- 'Manufactured' — mobile home, modular home, or manufactured housing (often requires specialty policy, not standard HO3)
- 'Other' — does not fit the above categories (e.g., concrete, hybrid, unique construction)
Map common caller terms:
- "wood" / "wood-frame" / "vinyl siding" → 'Frame' (vinyl siding is just exterior cladding over a frame structure)
- "brick" → 'Brick'
- "log cabin" → 'LogHome'
- "double-wide" / "trailer" / "mobile home" → 'Manufactured'
If unknown or declined, output 'Other'.
Note: 'Manufactured' homes typically require a specialty policy form (not standard HO3). If detected, flag for underwriter routing.
Output only the exact value. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: HO_SquareFootage
variables.description:
Extract the home's total above-ground living square footage as an integer.
- "2,500 square feet" → 2500
- "About 1,800" → 1800
- "Around 3,000 sq ft" → 3000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If the caller includes basement square footage, exclude it (HO3 typically rates on above-ground living space only — finished basement is captured separately as a special feature)
- If the caller only knows the lot size in acres, output 0 — that's not the same as home square footage
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: HO_Hazards
variables.description:
Extract any of the following hazards present at the home as a comma-separated list.
Use these exact labels:
- 'Pool' — in-ground or above-ground swimming pool (significant liability exposure; underwriter alert if no fencing)
- 'Trampoline' — backyard trampoline (auto-MEDIUM risk; some carriers require it to be removed before binding)
- 'Dog:<breed>' — caller has a dog. Append the breed: 'Dog:GoldenRetriever', 'Dog:PitBull', 'Dog:Mixed'
- 'Woodstove' — woodstove or wood-burning insert (different from a fireplace; higher fire risk)
- 'Fireplace' — gas or wood-burning fireplace (lower risk than woodstove)
- 'None' — no hazards present
Examples:
- 'Pool, Dog:Labrador' — has a pool and a Labrador
- 'Trampoline, Dog:PitBull, Woodstove' — multiple hazards
- 'None' — no hazards
For dog breed:
- If caller specifies the breed, capture it (e.g., 'Dog:GoldenRetriever', 'Dog:GermanShepherd', 'Dog:Rottweiler')
- If caller says "mixed breed" or "mutt", output 'Dog:Mixed'
- If caller has multiple dogs, list each: 'Dog:Labrador, Dog:Poodle'
- If caller mentions a dog but no breed, output 'Dog:Unknown'
Underwriter alert breeds (these trigger mandatory review): Pit Bull, Rottweiler, German Shepherd, Doberman, Chow Chow, Akita, Wolf-hybrid. Capture the breed honestly even when in this list.
If multiple hazards apply, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: HO_ClaimHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has had any homeowners claims (water damage, fire, theft, weather, liability — anything filed against a homeowners policy) in the past 5 years, including a count if Yes.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes: 'Yes: <count>' where <count> is the number of distinct claim events (e.g., 'Yes: 1', 'Yes: 2')
- Treat each separate event as one count regardless of dollar amount (a single roof claim = 1, two separate water claims = 2)
- Include claims even if the caller didn't end up filing them with the insurer (caller mentioning "we had a flood but paid out of pocket" still indicates loss exposure — output 'Yes: 1')
- If the caller is unsure of the count but had claims, output 'Yes: 1' as a conservative minimum
- If unknown or declined, output 'No'
Output only 'No' or 'Yes: <number>'. Do not output any other text or punctuation beyond the colon and digit.

variables.name: HO_SpecialFeatures
variables.description:
Extract any unusual features about the home that affect underwriting as a comma-separated list using these exact labels: 'Historic', 'LogHome', 'Lakefront', 'Coastal', 'WildfireZone', 'Earthquake', 'FinishedBasement', 'DetachedStructures', 'SolarPanels', 'PoolHouse', 'Other'.
- 'Historic' — historic property (registered or recognised historic home, typically pre-1900 with preservation status)
- 'LogHome' — log construction (may overlap with HO_ConstructionType but worth flagging here)
- 'Lakefront' — home is on or directly adjacent to a lake (water exposure, boat storage, dock)
- 'Coastal' — home is in a coastal zone (hurricane, flood exposure)
- 'WildfireZone' — home is in a wildfire-prone area (Western U.S. mountainous regions, brush areas)
- 'Earthquake' — home is in a seismic zone (West Coast, central U.S.)
- 'FinishedBasement' — basement that's been finished into living space (adds value, not always covered in main dwelling figure)
- 'DetachedStructures' — separate garage, shed, workshop, barn, guest house (Coverage B in HO3)
- 'SolarPanels' — solar panel array (significant additional value, may need rider)
- 'PoolHouse' — separate poolhouse structure (often combines with Pool hazard)
- 'Other' — caller mentions a feature not in the list above (capture in next conversation turn)
Examples:
- 'FinishedBasement, DetachedStructures' — typical suburban home
- 'Lakefront, DetachedStructures, Historic' — older lake home with separate boathouse
- 'WildfireZone, SolarPanels' — modern home in wildfire territory
- 'None' — standard home, no unusual features
If multiple features apply, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

transition_condition.prompt: All Homeowners Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

variables.name: PA_VehicleCount
variables.description:
Extract the number of vehicles needing coverage on the policy as an integer.
- "Two cars" → 2
- "Just my truck" → 1
- "Me and my husband, three vehicles" → 3
- "Four — me, my wife, and the two kids' cars" → 4
- If the caller gives a range, output the higher end (need to cover all)
- If unknown or declined, output 1
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: PA_VehicleList
variables.description:
Extract the list of vehicles to be insured as a semicolon-separated string. Each vehicle entry follows the format: 'Year Make Model | Ownership'.
Ownership values: 'Owned', 'Leased', 'Financed'
Format examples:
- Single vehicle: '2022 Honda Civic | Owned'
- Multiple vehicles: '2022 Honda Civic | Owned; 2020 Toyota RAV4 | Financed; 2019 Ford F-150 | Leased'
Rules:
- Use proper title case for makes and models (e.g., 'Honda', not 'honda' or 'HONDA')
- Year is always 4 digits (e.g., '2022', not '22' or "'22")
- Order vehicles in the order the caller mentioned them
- If a make or model is unclear, output 'Unknown' for that field (e.g., '2020 Unknown Unknown | Owned')
- If ownership is unclear, default to 'Owned' (most common)
- If the caller gives the count but no specifics, output 'Pending - to be collected'
- If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'
Output only the semicolon-separated list. Do not output any framing text, brackets, or quotation marks beyond the pipe separator.

variables.name: PA_DriverCount
variables.description:
Extract the number of drivers to be listed on the policy as an integer.
- "Just me" → 1
- "Me and my wife" → 2
- "Three — me, my husband, and our teenager" → 3
- "Two adults plus our 17-year-old" → 3
- If unknown or declined, output the same value as PA_VehicleCount as a fallback
Note: All household members of legal driving age who will operate the vehicles should be listed. Excluded drivers (people in the household who will NOT drive) are typically captured separately and not counted here.
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: PA_DriverList
variables.description:
Extract the list of drivers as a semicolon-separated string. Each driver entry follows the format: 'Name | Age | YearsLicensed | History'.
History values: 'Clean' OR 'Yes:<count>' (count of tickets + accidents combined in last 3 years)
Format examples:
- Single driver: 'John Smith | 42 | 25 | Clean'
- Multiple drivers: 'John Smith | 42 | 25 | Clean; Jane Smith | 40 | 22 | Yes:1; Mike Smith | 17 | 1 | Clean'
Rules for each field:
- Name: full name in proper title case (e.g., 'John Smith')
- Age: integer, the driver's current age
- YearsLicensed: integer, number of years the driver has held a license (e.g., '25' for someone who got their license at age 17 and is now 42)
- History: 'Clean' if no tickets or accidents in last 3 years; 'Yes:<count>' if any incidents (tickets + accidents combined; 'Yes:1' for one ticket, 'Yes:3' for three combined)
If any field is unknown for a driver:
- Name unknown → 'Unknown'
- Age unknown → 0
- YearsLicensed unknown → 0
- History unknown → 'Clean' (conservative default; underwriting will verify via MVR pull)
If the caller mentions a DUI specifically, append ':DUI' to the History field (e.g., 'Yes:1:DUI'). DUIs trigger an underwriter alert and rate differently from standard violations.
If the caller gives a count but no specifics, output 'Pending - to be collected'.
Output only the semicolon-separated list. Do not output any framing text, brackets, or quotation marks beyond the pipe separator.

variables.name: PA_AnnualMileage
variables.description:
Extract the approximate annual mileage for each vehicle as a structured string. Mileage drives premium significantly — accuracy matters.
Format options:
- Single value (applies to all vehicles): '12000' or '15000'
- Per-vehicle: 'Vehicle1:12000|Vehicle2:8000|Vehicle3:5000' (use the order from PA_VehicleList)
Common caller phrasings:
- "About 12,000 miles a year, all of us" → '12000'
- "I drive 15K, my wife does 8K, the kid drives like 3K" → 'Vehicle1:15000|Vehicle2:8000|Vehicle3:3000'
- "Around 10 to 12 thousand" → '11000' (midpoint)
- "I work from home so probably 5,000" → '5000'
Standard mileage tiers for reference:
- Low usage: under 7,500 miles/year
- Standard: 7,500 to 15,000 miles/year (most common)
- High usage: 15,000 to 25,000 miles/year (commute or long trips)
- Very high: over 25,000 miles/year (auto-MEDIUM risk minimum)
If unknown or declined, output 12000 (industry-standard default).
Output only the integer or pipe-separated structured string. Do not output any text, commas, or units.

variables.name: PA_UseType
variables.description:
Classify the primary use of the vehicles as exactly one of these values: 'Commute', 'Pleasure', 'Business', or 'Mixed'.
- 'Commute' — primary use is commuting to/from work or school
- 'Pleasure' — primary use is non-work driving (errands, leisure, family activities)
- 'Business' — vehicle is used for business purposes (sales calls, deliveries, client visits) — note: heavy business use may require Commercial Auto, not Personal Auto
- 'Mixed' — vehicles are used for multiple purposes roughly equally
Map common caller phrasings:
- "Drive to work every day" → 'Commute'
- "Just running errands and weekend trips" → 'Pleasure'
- "I use it for sales calls" → 'Business' (flag for Commercial Auto evaluation)
- "Mix of commute and personal" → 'Mixed'
If multiple vehicles have different primary uses (e.g., one commuter, one weekend car), output 'Mixed'.
If 'Business' is selected and the use is heavy (>50% business mileage), this triggers an underwriter flag — Personal Auto may not adequately cover business use.
If unknown or declined, output 'Mixed'.
Output only the exact value 'Commute', 'Pleasure', 'Business', or 'Mixed'. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: PA_GarageLocation
variables.description:
Extract where the vehicles are garaged (parked overnight) as a structured string.
Format options:
- If garaged at the caller's home address: 'OwnerAddress'
- If garaged at a different address: 'Different:<full address>' (e.g., 'Different:123 Storage Lane, Madison, Wisconsin 53703')
- If no garage exists (parked on street, driveway, parking lot): 'NoGarage'
Common caller phrasings:
- "Yes, in my garage at home" → 'OwnerAddress'
- "Garage at my house" → 'OwnerAddress'
- "Parked in my driveway" → 'NoGarage' (technically not in a garage, even if at home)
- "I park on the street" → 'NoGarage'
- "It's at my vacation house in Door County" → 'Different:<that address>'
Note: 'NoGarage' (street/driveway parking) typically increases comprehensive premiums slightly due to higher theft/weather exposure.
If multiple vehicles have different garage locations, output the location of the majority (and flag in conversation for caller confirmation).
If unknown or declined, default to 'OwnerAddress' (most common).
Output only the structured string. Do not output any framing text or quotation marks.

variables.name: PA_CoveragePrefs
variables.description:
Extract the caller's preferences for coverage limits and deductibles as a structured pipe-separated string.
Format: 'Liability:<limits>|Collision:<deductible>|Comprehensive:<deductible>|UninsuredMotorist:<limits>'
Liability limits format:
- Split limits: '<per_person>/<per_accident>/<property>' (e.g., '100/300/100' for $100K/$300K/$100K)
- Combined single limit: 'CSL:<amount>' (e.g., 'CSL:500000')
- Common values: '50/100/50', '100/300/100' (most common), '250/500/250', '500/500/500'
Deductible values: integer in dollars (e.g., '500', '1000', '2500')
Examples:
- 'Liability:100/300/100|Collision:1000|Comprehensive:500|UninsuredMotorist:100/300' — typical mid-range coverage
- 'Liability:250/500/250|Collision:500|Comprehensive:500|UninsuredMotorist:250/500' — higher coverage
- 'Liability:50/100/50|Collision:1000|Comprehensive:1000|UninsuredMotorist:50/100' — minimum coverage
Common caller phrasings:
- "Standard coverage" → 'Liability:100/300/100|Collision:1000|Comprehensive:500|UninsuredMotorist:100/300'
- "Just the minimum" → use state minimums (varies by state; default to '50/100/50' for Wisconsin)
- "Full coverage" → typically means liability + collision + comprehensive at standard limits
- "I want a high deductible" → 'Collision:2500|Comprehensive:2500'
If the caller doesn't specify and asks for recommendations, output 'Liability:100/300/100|Collision:1000|Comprehensive:500|UninsuredMotorist:100/300' (industry standard).
If unknown or declined, output the standard recommendation above.
Output only the structured pipe-separated string. Do not output any framing text, currency symbols, or quotation marks.

transition_condition.prompt: All Personal Auto Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

variables.name: DF_PropertyAddress
variables.description:
Extract the address of the rental/non-owner-occupied property as a single-line string.
Format: 'Street Number Street Name, City, State Zip' — e.g., '128 Oak Avenue, Madison, Wisconsin 53703'
- Use proper title case for street and city names
- Spell out the state name in full ('Wisconsin', not 'WI')
- Include the ZIP code if provided
- Include unit number if applicable: '128 Oak Avenue, Unit 3, Madison, Wisconsin 53703'
- This is the RENTAL property address, NOT the owner's home address — it should typically be different from OwnerAddress
- If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'
Note: For Dwelling Fire, the property address is almost always different from OwnerAddress (the caller doesn't live there). If the caller seems to give OwnerAddress, gently confirm: "Just to clarify — that's the address of the rental property itself, correct?"
Output only the address string. Do not output any framing text, labels, or quotation marks.

variables.name: DF_DwellingValue
variables.description:
Extract the rental property's replacement cost (cost to rebuild from scratch) in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$200,000" → 200000
- "Around 350K" → 350000
- "Half a million to rebuild" → 500000
- "I paid 180" → 180000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If the caller gives "what I paid" or market value, capture that and flag in confirmation step (replacement cost is typically 80-100% of market value)
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Rental property replacement costs are often lower per square foot than owner-occupied homes due to simpler construction and finishes. Don't be surprised by lower numbers than typical Homeowners.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: DF_OccupancyStatus
variables.description:
Classify the current occupancy status of the rental property as exactly one of these values: 'Rented', 'Vacant', 'Seasonal', or 'BetweenTenants'.
- 'Rented' — currently has a tenant under lease, occupied (most common, lowest risk)
- 'Vacant' — property is currently empty with no tenant and no plans to occupy in the next 60 days (significant underwriting flag — vacant >30 days requires review)
- 'Seasonal' — property is occupied only seasonally (vacation rental, summer home, ski cabin) — this is a different risk profile from year-round rental
- 'BetweenTenants' — property is currently empty but actively being rented to a new tenant within 30 days (lower risk than Vacant)
Map common caller phrasings:
- "Yes, currently rented" → 'Rented'
- "It's vacant right now, looking for tenants" → 'BetweenTenants' (if actively listing) or 'Vacant' (if not listing)
- "Empty for the past 6 months" → 'Vacant' (long-term vacancy = underwriter alert)
- "Summer rental, occupied May to October" → 'Seasonal'
- "Airbnb / short-term rental" → 'Seasonal' (short-term rentals often need specialty short-term rental policy, not standard DP-3 — flag)
If unknown or declined, output 'Vacant' (most conservative for underwriting — forces review).
Output only the exact value. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: DF_YearsAsRental
variables.description:
Extract the number of years the property has been used as a rental as an integer.
- "Five years" → 5
- "Just bought it last year, started renting it out" → 1
- "We've rented it since 2010" → compute from 2010 to current year
- "Decades" → 20 (conservative)
- "Brand new rental, never been rented" → 0
- If the property was previously owner-occupied and only recently became a rental, output the number of years since it became a rental (not the total years owned)
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Properties that have been rentals for less than 12 months sometimes need a "new venture" rate factor applied. Long-term rentals (10+ years) often have established loss patterns that get factored into pricing.
Output only an integer. Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: DF_YearBuilt
variables.description:
Extract the year the rental property was originally constructed as a 4-digit integer.
- "1985" → 1985
- "Built in '92" → 1992
- "Around 1970" → 1970
- "Late 1800s" → 1890
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint as an integer
- If the caller mentions a major renovation year, capture the original build year, not the renovation year
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Rental properties built before 1950 may have outdated electrical/plumbing systems and trigger underwriter review. Pre-1900 construction often requires a specialty older-home rider.
Output only a 4-digit integer or 0. Do not output any text, abbreviations, or apostrophes.

variables.name: DF_ConstructionType
variables.description:
Classify the rental property's construction type as exactly one of these values: 'Frame', 'Brick', 'Stucco', 'Stone', 'LogHome', 'Manufactured', or 'Other'.
- 'Frame' — wood-frame construction (most common)
- 'Brick' — brick veneer or solid brick exterior
- 'Stucco' — stucco exterior over wood frame or masonry
- 'Stone' — natural stone or stone veneer exterior
- 'LogHome' — log construction (less common as rental, may need specialty rider)
- 'Manufactured' — mobile home, modular home, or manufactured housing (often requires specialty policy form)
- 'Other' — does not fit the above categories (e.g., concrete, hybrid)
Map common caller terms:
- "wood" / "wood-frame" / "vinyl siding" → 'Frame'
- "brick" → 'Brick'
- "log cabin" → 'LogHome'
- "double-wide" / "trailer" / "mobile home" → 'Manufactured'
If unknown or declined, output 'Other'.
Note: 'Manufactured' rentals typically require a specialty mobile home policy form, not standard DP-3. If detected, flag for underwriter routing.
Output only the exact value. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: DF_TenantPolicies
variables.description:
Extract the caller's tenant screening practices and renters' insurance requirements as a structured string. These are key underwriting factors for rental properties.
Format: 'Screening:<value>|RentersInsurance:<value>'
Screening values:
- 'Yes' — caller screens tenants (credit check, background check, references, income verification)
- 'Basic' — caller does some screening but not comprehensive (e.g., references only)
- 'No' — caller does not screen tenants
RentersInsurance values:
- 'Required' — caller requires tenants to carry renters' insurance and verifies it
- 'Suggested' — caller suggests but doesn't enforce renters' insurance
- 'No' — caller does not require renters' insurance
Examples:
- 'Screening:Yes|RentersInsurance:Required' — best-practice landlord (lowest risk)
- 'Screening:Basic|RentersInsurance:Suggested' — typical small landlord
- 'Screening:No|RentersInsurance:No' — informal arrangement (HIGH risk flag)
- 'Screening:Yes|RentersInsurance:No' — partial best practices
If unknown or declined, output 'Screening:No|RentersInsurance:No' (most conservative for underwriting).
Output only the structured string. Do not output any other text or formatting.

variables.name: DF_ClaimHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has had any prior claims at this specific rental property (water damage, fire, theft, vandalism, tenant-caused damage, liability) in the past 5 years, including a count if Yes.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes: 'Yes: <count>' where <count> is the number of distinct claim events at this property (e.g., 'Yes: 1', 'Yes: 2')
- Treat each separate event as one count regardless of dollar amount
- This refers to claims at THIS property only, not at other rentals or the caller's primary residence
- If the caller is unsure of the count but had claims, output 'Yes: 1' as a conservative minimum
- If unknown or declined, output 'No'
Note: Tenant-caused damage that the caller paid out of pocket (didn't file with insurer) still counts — output 'Yes: 1'. Loss exposure is what matters.
Output only 'No' or 'Yes: <number>'. Do not output any other text or punctuation beyond the colon and digit.

variables.name: DF_OwnershipStructure
variables.description:
Classify how the rental property is legally owned as exactly one of these values: 'Personal', 'LLC', 'Trust', 'Corporation', 'Partnership', or 'Other'.
- 'Personal' — owned in the caller's personal name(s) (or jointly with a spouse)
- 'LLC' — owned by a limited liability company (most common for serious landlords)
- 'Trust' — owned by a trust (revocable living trust, family trust, etc.)
- 'Corporation' — owned by a C-corp or S-corp
- 'Partnership' — owned by a general or limited partnership
- 'Other' — owned by some other entity type (REIT, syndicate, foreign entity)
Map common caller phrasings:
- "In my name" / "Personally" → 'Personal'
- "We have an LLC for our rentals" → 'LLC'
- "Owned by our family trust" → 'Trust'
Note: 'LLC' or 'Corporation' ownership may require a Commercial Property policy instead of DP-3 (Dwelling Fire is technically a personal-lines product). This is an important underwriter routing flag.
If unknown or declined, output 'Personal' (most common for individual landlords).
Output only the exact value. Do not output any other text.

transition_condition.prompt: All Dwelling Fire Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

instruction.text:
You are managing a multi-policy bundle. {BundleQueue} contains the list of policies to collect.

 
Open with (first iteration only):
"Great — for the bundle, I'll work through one policy at a time. We'll start with [first policy], then move on. Sound good?"
 
# LOGIC
 
For each policy in {BundleQueue}:
  1. Set {CurrentPolicy} = next policy in queue.
  2. Transition to that policy's intake node (N9-N19).
  3. After that node completes, return to N20.
  4. If queue has more, say briefly: "Perfect — that's [previous policy] done. Now let's move to [next]."
  5. Repeat until queue empty.
 
When queue is empty:
  Say: "We've covered everything. Let me run the numbers on your bundle now."
  Transition to N21 (Risk Scoring).
 
# RESPONSE STYLE
- Keep transitions between policies LIGHT — one bridging sentence max.
- Acknowledge progress: "That's two of three done."
- Empathy hooks still active — if caller fatigues, offer to break the call.
 
# DATA CARRY-OVER
- Owner contact info (N8 outputs) carries to all bundled policies — never re-ask.
- Profile data (N4 outputs) carries to all bundled policies — never re-ask.
- Property addresses may overlap (Homeowners + Personal Auto often same address) — confirm rather than re-ask: "Same address as the home?"

variables.name: FO_PropertyAddress
variables.description:
Extract the address of the farm property as a single-line string.
Format: 'Street Number Street Name, City, State Zip' — e.g., 'W4521 County Road A, Cumberland, Wisconsin 54829'
- Use proper title case for street and city names
- Spell out the state name in full ('Wisconsin', not 'WI')
- Include the ZIP code if provided
- Farm addresses commonly use rural route format (e.g., 'W4521 County Road A', 'N7823 State Highway 13', 'Rural Route 2 Box 142') — preserve as given
- The farm address is often the same as OwnerAddress for owner-operated farms
- If the caller's farm has multiple addresses (e.g., main farm and a separate parcel), capture the primary residence/operations address
- If unknown or declined, output 'Unknown'
Output only the address string. Do not output any framing text, labels, or quotation marks.

variables.name: FO_Acreage
variables.description:
Extract the total size of the farm property in acres as a numeric value.
- "80 acres" → 80
- "About 240" → 240
- "Half a section" → 320 (a section = 640 acres in the U.S. survey system)
- "Quarter section" → 160
- "1,200 acres" → 1200
- "About 5 to 10 acres" → 7.5 (midpoint)
- "Hobby farm, just 3 acres" → 3
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Farm size affects pricing significantly. Common ranges:
- Under 10 acres → typically a hobby farm or small homestead
- 10-160 acres → small family farm
- 160-1000 acres → mid-size family farm
- Over 1000 acres → large commercial operation (may exceed Farmowners appetite)
Output only a number. Decimals are acceptable (e.g., 7.5, 240.25). Do not output any text, units, or symbols.

variables.name: FO_OperationType
variables.description:
Classify the type of farm operation as a comma-separated list using these exact labels: 'Crops', 'Livestock', 'Dairy', 'Poultry', 'Mixed', 'Hobby', 'Equestrian', or 'Other'.
- 'Crops' — crop production (corn, soybeans, wheat, hay, vegetables, fruit, etc.)
- 'Livestock' — beef cattle, hogs, sheep, goats (animals raised for meat or breeding, NOT dairy)
- 'Dairy' — dairy operation (milk-producing cows or goats) — distinct from beef cattle
- 'Poultry' — chickens, turkeys, ducks (eggs or meat)
- 'Mixed' — combination of crops and livestock together (very common in Wisconsin/Midwest)
- 'Hobby' — small-scale, non-commercial farming (caller doesn't depend on it as primary income)
- 'Equestrian' — horses (boarding, breeding, training, riding stable)
- 'Other' — specialty operations (vineyards, orchards, fish farming, beekeeping, Christmas tree farms, etc.)
Examples:
- 'Crops, Livestock' — typical Midwest mixed farm
- 'Dairy' — dedicated dairy operation
- 'Crops' — grain farm
- 'Hobby, Equestrian' — small property with a few horses
- 'Other' — vineyard, fishery, or specialty operation
If multiple types apply, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'Mixed' (most common for small-to-mid farms).
Note: 'Equestrian' operations involving boarding or lessons usually require additional liability coverage. 'Other' specialty operations may need a Specialty Farm policy form rather than standard Farmowners.
Output only the comma-separated list. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: FO_DwellingValue
variables.description:
Extract the replacement cost of the farm dwelling (the residence on the farm property) in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
- "$250,000" → 250000
- "Around 180K" → 180000
- "Half a million" → 500000
- "Old farmhouse, maybe 150 thousand" → 150000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If the caller has multiple dwellings on the farm (main house + tenant house + farmhand quarters), output the value of the primary residence; flag the additional dwellings in conversation for separate scheduling
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Farmhouse replacement costs are often lower than suburban homes due to simpler construction. Don't be surprised by lower numbers.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: FO_EquipmentValue
variables.description:
Extract the total value of farm equipment, machinery, and implements in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
Equipment includes: tractors, combines, balers, plows, planters, sprayers, irrigation systems, milking equipment, grain bins, augers, livestock handling equipment, ATVs/UTVs used in farming, etc.
- "$50,000 in equipment" → 50000
- "Around 200K total" → 200000
- "Just a small tractor and some hand tools, maybe 30 grand" → 30000
- "Half a million dollars" → 500000
- "1.2 mil" → 1200000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Farm equipment often appreciates significantly above book value due to current new-equipment prices. Replacement cost is typically higher than depreciated/book value.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: FO_LivestockValue
variables.description:
Extract the total value of livestock on the farm in U.S. dollars as a numeric value (no currency symbols, no commas, no text).
Livestock includes all animals on the farm: cattle (beef and dairy), hogs, sheep, goats, poultry, horses, and any other commercial animals.
- "$80,000 worth of cattle" → 80000
- "About 50K total" → 50000
- "We don't have any livestock" → 0
- "Half a million in dairy cows" → 500000
- If the caller gives a range, output the midpoint
- If FO_OperationType doesn't include 'Livestock', 'Dairy', 'Poultry', or 'Equestrian', output 0
- If unknown or declined, output 0
Note: Livestock values fluctuate seasonally. Capture the caller's current best estimate. Underwriting will use a per-head average for most species.
Output only a number. Do not output any currency symbols, commas, units, or text.

variables.name: FO_FarmEmployees
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has farm employees or seasonal workers, including counts.
Format options:
- If no employees: 'None'
- If only seasonal: 'Seasonal:<count>' (e.g., 'Seasonal:5')
- If only full-time: 'FullTime:<count>' (e.g., 'FullTime:2')
- If both: 'FullTime:<count>, Seasonal:<count>' (e.g., 'FullTime:2, Seasonal:8')
Definitions:
- 'FullTime' — year-round paid employees, including farm hands and management staff
- 'Seasonal' — temporary workers during planting, harvest, or peak periods (often migrant labor or local hires)
- Family members who work the farm without formal pay are NOT counted as employees
Examples:
- 'None' — owner-operated, no paid help
- 'Seasonal:8' — small farm with 8 harvest workers each fall
- 'FullTime:2' — mid-size farm with 2 year-round farm hands
- 'FullTime:5, Seasonal:15' — large operation with year-round crew plus seasonal labor
Common caller phrasings:
- "Just family" → 'None'
- "A couple guys help out during harvest" → 'Seasonal:2' (estimate)
- "I have one full-time hand" → 'FullTime:1'
- "We hire 10 to 15 for the season" → 'Seasonal:13' (midpoint)
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Note: Any farm employees (full-time or seasonal) typically trigger a Workers' Compensation requirement. Most states mandate WC coverage for farm employees, with thresholds varying by state. This routes to a separate Workers' Comp recommendation.
Output only the structured string. Do not output any other text or formatting.

variables.name: FO_LossHistory
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller has had any farm-related losses in the past 5 years, including the type and count if Yes.
Format:
- If No: 'No'
- If Yes: 'Yes:<type>:<count>' where <type> is the loss category and <count> is the number of distinct events
Loss types (use these exact labels):
- 'Crop' — crop loss from weather, disease, or pests (drought, hail, flood, blight)
- 'Livestock' — livestock loss from disease, predation, weather, or accident
- 'Equipment' — farm equipment damage or theft
- 'Building' — barn, silo, or farm building damage (fire, wind, collapse)
- 'Liability' — third-party injury on the farm (farm visitor, agritourism customer, neighbor)
- 'Mixed' — combination of multiple types
Format examples:
- 'No' — clean history
- 'Yes:Crop:1' — one prior crop loss
- 'Yes:Equipment:2' — two equipment damage/theft events
- 'Yes:Mixed:3' — three losses across different types
If multiple specific types occurred, you can list each: 'Yes:Crop:1, Yes:Livestock:1' — but if more than 2 types or unclear, default to 'Yes:Mixed:<total count>'.
If the caller is unsure of the count but had losses, output 'Yes:Mixed:1' as a conservative minimum.
If unknown or declined, output 'No'.
Note: Federal crop insurance claims (USDA RMA) are typically separate from Farmowners and are NOT counted here. Only losses paid (or that should have been paid) by the Farmowners policy itself.
Output only the structured string. Do not output any other text or formatting.

variables.name: FO_DirectSales
variables.description:
Extract whether the caller engages in direct-to-consumer sales activities as a comma-separated list. These activities create significant additional liability exposure beyond standard farm operations.
Use these exact labels:
- 'FarmStand' — roadside farm stand selling produce or goods (visitors come to the property)
- 'Agritourism' — paid farm visits, tours, weddings, events, corn mazes, pumpkin patches, hayrides
- 'UPick' — customers pick produce themselves (apples, berries, pumpkins, Christmas trees) — HIGH liability
- 'FarmersMarket' — caller sells off-farm at farmers markets (lower on-property exposure)
- 'CSA' — Community Supported Agriculture subscription model (caller delivers or customers pick up)
- 'OnlineSales' — caller sells products online (eggs, honey, jams, meat) and ships
- 'None' — caller sells only to wholesalers, processors, or middlemen — no consumer interaction on the farm
Examples:
- 'None' — traditional commodity farmer, sells only to grain elevator or co-op
- 'FarmStand, FarmersMarket' — typical small farm with multiple direct outlets
- 'UPick, Agritourism, FarmStand' — significant agritourism operation (auto-HIGH liability minimum)
- 'CSA, OnlineSales' — modern small farm selling through CSA boxes and an online store
If multiple types apply, list all separated by commas in any order.
If unknown or declined, output 'None'.
Note: 'Agritourism' and 'UPick' both involve members of the public on the farm property and create significant slip/fall and animal-related liability exposure. Standard Farmowners policies often exclude or sub-limit these activities — if detected, flag for underwriter to confirm coverage scope or recommend agritourism-specific liability rider.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any other text, brackets, or quotation marks.

transition_condition.prompt: All Farmowners Intake-specific underwriting questions answered.
If this was one policy in a bundle, return to N20 for next policy.
Caller mentions a past claim, loss, or hardship requiring empathy turn.

variables.name: CurrentPolicy
variables.description:
Maintain the active policy being processed in the current iteration of the bundle loop. Updates each time the loop advances to the next policy.
Use the exact standard policy names:
- 'Workers' Compensation'
- 'General Liability'
- 'Commercial Auto'
- 'Commercial Property'
- 'Umbrella / Excess Liability'
- 'BOP'
- 'Professional Liability'
- 'Homeowners'
- 'Personal Auto'
- 'Dwelling Fire'
- 'Farmowners'
Logic for setting CurrentPolicy:
- On first arrival at N20: set CurrentPolicy to the FIRST policy in BundleQueue
- After completing each policy intake node (N9-N19) and returning to N20: set CurrentPolicy to the NEXT un-processed policy in BundleQueue
- When BundleQueue has been fully processed (all policies completed): set CurrentPolicy to 'Empty'
Note: This variable was previously written by N7 for single-policy paths. In the bundle path, N20 takes ownership and rewrites it on each loop iteration.
Output only the exact policy name or 'Empty'. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: BundleData
variables.description:
Maintain an aggregated record of all data collected across the bundle's policy intake nodes. This is the consolidated dataset that downstream nodes (N21 Risk Scoring, N22 Premium Calc, N23 Validation) will read from.
Format: a JSON-like structured string with one object per completed policy.
Example after Homeowners + Personal Auto bundle:
{"Homeowners":{"PropertyAddress":"742 Maple Drive, Madison, Wisconsin 53703","DwellingValue":400000,"YearBuilt":1985,"ConstructionType":"Frame","SquareFootage":2200,"Hazards":"None","ClaimHistory":"No","SpecialFeatures":"None"},"PersonalAuto":{"VehicleCount":2,"VehicleList":"2022 Honda Civic | Owned; 2020 Toyota RAV4 | Financed","DriverCount":2,"DriverList":"John Smith | 42 | 25 | Clean; Jane Smith | 40 | 22 | Clean","AnnualMileage":"12000","UseType":"Commute","GarageLocation":"OwnerAddress","CoveragePrefs":"Liability:100/300/100|Collision:1000|Comprehensive:500|UninsuredMotorist:100/300"}}
Logic:
- On first arrival at N20: initialize BundleData as an empty JSON object: '{}'
- After completing each policy intake node (N9-N19) and returning to N20: append a new entry to BundleData using the policy name as the key and an object containing all that policy's WRITE variables as the value
- Use the variable names from each policy node WITHOUT the policy prefix (e.g., HO_DwellingValue becomes "DwellingValue" in the BundleData entry, since the prefix is implicit from the parent key)
Use the exact standard policy names as keys: 'Homeowners', 'PersonalAuto', 'WorkersComp', 'GeneralLiability', 'CommercialAuto', 'CommercialProperty', 'Umbrella', 'BOP', 'ProfessionalLiability', 'DwellingFire', 'Farmowners'.
Output only the structured JSON-like string. Do not output any framing text, code blocks, or quotation marks beyond what's in the JSON itself.

transition_condition.prompt: {BundleQueue} is empty — all bundled policies have completed intake.

transcript.content: [All policy data clean, no claims]
Your profile looks really clean — that should put you in our most competitive pricing tier. Let me put a number range together for you.
[2 prior claims + DUI]

There are a couple of items our underwriting team will want to take a closer look at, which is pretty normal when there are recent claims and incidents on the record. We'll still get you a quote range — just with the caveat that final pricing depends on their review.

instruction.text:
You are computing the risk level. The caller does NOT need to hear the calculation — just the outcome, framed naturally.

 
# INPUTS
All policy-specific risk flags collected in N9-N19. Plus:
- {PriorCancellation} from N5
- {AssetBracket} from N4
 
# COMPUTATION (executed silently, then announced)
 
LOW:
- No prior claims, no flagged risk factors, standard exposure profile.
- Default for new business with clean history.
 
MEDIUM:
- 1-2 risk flags from policy intake (e.g., older home, single prior claim, fleet > 5 vehicles).
- {PriorCancellation} = Yes with no other flags.
 
HIGH:
- 3+ risk flags, OR any single hard flag (DUI, restricted operations, EMR > 1.20, multiple claims, off-appetite industry).
- Triggers underwriter alert email downstream.
 
# DELIVERY
 
Frame the risk level naturally — never use the words "low risk" or "high risk" with the caller (sounds clinical/judgmental). Translate:
 
LOW → "Your profile looks really clean — that should put you in our most competitive pricing tier."
MEDIUM → "Your profile is solid — we'll have a good range for you, with some standard underwriting review."
HIGH → "There are a couple of items our underwriting team will want to take a closer look at, which is normal for [reason]. We'll still get you a quote range, just with the caveat that final pricing depends on their review."
 
# EXIT
Set {RiskLevel} and transition to N22 (Quote Estimate).

variables.name: RiskLevel
variables.description:
Compute the caller's overall risk level based on all collected data as exactly one of: 'Low', 'Medium', or 'High'.
This is a COMPUTED variable — derive it from upstream variables, not from caller speech.
EVALUATION RULES:
Output 'High' if ANY ONE of these hard flags is present:
- Any DUI in last 5 years (PA_DriverList contains ':DUI')
- WC_EMR > 1.20
- 3 or more total risk flags from any combination below
- WC_ClaimHistory has 3+ claims OR PL_ClaimHistory contains 'Disciplinary'
- Off-appetite industry (caller in cannabis, adult, logging, roofing, etc. — verify against WMIC's prohibited list)
- High-hazard combinations: long-haul trucking + driver violations, hazmat cargo, multiple liability claims
Output 'Medium' if 1-2 soft flags are present (any combination of the below):
- PriorCancellation = 'Yes' (from N5)
- Single prior claim across any policy (HO_ClaimHistory, GL_ClaimHistory, CP_LossHistory, etc.)
- Fleet > 5 vehicles (CA_VehicleCount > 5)
- Building > 50 years old (HO_YearBuilt < current_year - 50, or CP_YearBuilt < current_year - 50)
- Aggressive dog breed (HO_Hazards contains 'Dog:PitBull', 'Dog:Rottweiler', etc.)
- Pool without confirmed fencing (HO_Hazards contains 'Pool')
- Annual mileage > 20,000 (PA_AnnualMileage exceeds 20000)
- High-traffic premises (GL_FootTraffic > 500)
- Coastal or wildfire zone (HO_SpecialFeatures contains 'Coastal' or 'WildfireZone')
Output 'Low' if NONE of the above flags are present:
- No prior claims, no cancellations, no DUI
- Standard exposure profile across all collected data
- Default for new business with clean history
Edge cases:
- Exactly 2 soft flags → 'Medium' (NOT 'High')
- 3 soft flags → 'High'
- 1 hard flag → 'High' regardless of soft flag count
Output only the exact value 'Low', 'Medium', or 'High'. Do not output any other text.

variables.name: RiskFactorList
variables.description:
Compute a list of every risk factor that was triggered for this caller. This is used by the downstream alert email (N25) to tell underwriters exactly why the risk level was set.
Format: comma-separated list of risk factor labels using these exact labels:
Hard flags:
- 'DUI' — DUI present in driver history
- 'EMR_HIGH' — Workers' Comp EMR > 1.20
- 'CLAIMS_MULTIPLE' — 3 or more claims across any policy
- 'DISCIPLINARY' — Professional Liability disciplinary action
- 'OFF_APPETITE' — Industry not in WMIC's appetite
- 'HAZMAT' — Commercial Auto hauling hazardous materials
- 'LONG_HAUL_VIOLATIONS' — Long-haul trucking with driver violations
Soft flags:
- 'PRIOR_CANCELLATION' — PriorCancellation = 'Yes' from N5
- 'PRIOR_CLAIM' — Single prior claim in any policy
- 'LARGE_FLEET' — Vehicle count > 5
- 'OLDER_BUILDING' — Building older than 50 years
- 'AGGRESSIVE_BREED' — Restricted dog breed
- 'POOL' — Pool present at residence
- 'HIGH_MILEAGE' — Annual mileage > 20,000
- 'HIGH_FOOT_TRAFFIC' — GL_FootTraffic > 500
- 'COASTAL_ZONE' — Property in coastal area
- 'WILDFIRE_ZONE' — Property in wildfire area
- 'AGRITOURISM' — Farm with agritourism/U-Pick operations
- 'FARM_EMPLOYEES' — Farm with paid employees
- 'VACANT_PROPERTY' — Dwelling Fire on vacant property
- 'LLC_OWNERSHIP' — Dwelling Fire owned by LLC
- 'NO_TENANT_SCREENING' — Dwelling Fire with no tenant screening
- 'AGED_HOME' — Pre-1950 construction
- 'MANUFACTURED_HOME' — Manufactured housing construction
If no factors triggered, output 'None'.
Examples:
- 'None' — clean profile
- 'PRIOR_CLAIM, OLDER_BUILDING' — two soft flags (Medium risk)
- 'DUI, PRIOR_CANCELLATION' — one hard flag + one soft (High risk due to DUI)
- 'EMR_HIGH, CLAIMS_MULTIPLE, DISCIPLINARY' — three hard flags (High risk, severe)
List all triggered factors separated by commas. Order doesn't matter.
Output only the comma-separated list or 'None'. Do not output any framing text, brackets, or quotation marks.

variables.name: AlertFlag
variables.description:
Set the underwriter alert flag based on RiskLevel. This drives whether N25 sends an alert email to the underwriting team.
Logic:
- If RiskLevel is 'High' → output 'Yes'
- If RiskLevel is 'Medium' OR 'Low' → output 'No'
This is a derived flag — it should be deterministic based on RiskLevel. Do not use any other inputs.
Output only the exact value 'Yes' or 'No'. Do not output any other text.

transition_condition.prompt: Risk level computed and {RiskLevel} variable set.

transcript.content: [After Personal Auto intake, low risk]

Based on what you've shared, you're looking at roughly $1,200 to $1,500 a year. Just so you know, this is a ballpark — our underwriting team will confirm the final number — but it gives us a good starting point. Want me to walk you through what's included before I put the summary together?

instruction.text:
You are delivering the pre-quote premium range. Compute QuoteLow and QuoteHigh based on the reference rates below — never leave them at 0.

# POLICY NAME MAPPING
If SelectedPolicy does not exactly match a Reference Rate label, 
use the closest match:
- "Auto", "Personal Auto", "Car Insurance" → Personal Auto (1200)
- "Home", "Home Insurance", "Homeowners Insurance" → Homeowners (1800)
- "Farm", "Farm Insurance", "Farmowners" → Farm Insurance (3000)
- "Dwelling Fire", "Dwelling Fire Insurance", "DF" → Dwelling Fire (900)
- "Commercial", "BOP", "Business Insurance" → Commercial Insurance (2400)
- "Umbrella", "Umbrella Insurance", "Excess Liability" → Umbrella Insurance (600)
If still no match, default base_premium = 1500 and flag in RiskFactorList 
as "rate-lookup-fallback".

# REFERENCE RATES (base premium per policy, annualized USD)
Personal Auto: 1200
Homeowners: 1800
Farm Insurance: 3000
Dwelling Fire: 900
Commercial Insurance: 2400
Umbrella Insurance: 600
# RISK MULTIPLIER (based on RiskLevel)
Low: 0.85
Medium: 1.05
High: 1.35

# COMPUTATION (do this silently before speaking)
Step 1: Look up base_premium from REFERENCE RATES based on SelectedPolicy.
Step 2: Look up risk_multiplier from RISK MULTIPLIER based on RiskLevel. If RiskLevel is not set, default to 1.0.
Step 3: Compute:
  QuoteLow = base_premium × risk_multiplier × 0.90
  QuoteHigh = base_premium × risk_multiplier × 1.15
Step 4: Round both to the nearest 25.
Step 5: Set QuoteFrequency = "annual"
Step 6: If BundleFlag = "Yes", set BundleSavingsPercent = 10. Otherwise 0.
Step 7: If BundleFlag = "Yes":
  QuoteLow  = QuoteLow  × 0.90 (apply bundle discount)
  QuoteHigh = QuoteHigh × 0.90
  Round both to nearest 25.

# WORKED EXAMPLES
Example 1 — Personal Auto, Low risk:
  base = 1200, mult = 0.85
  QuoteLow = 1200 × 0.85 × 0.90 = 918 → 925
  QuoteHigh = 1200 × 0.85 × 1.15 = 1173 → 1175
Example 2 — Homeowners, Medium risk:
  base = 1800, mult = 1.05
  QuoteLow = 1800 × 1.05 × 0.90 = 1701 → 1700
  QuoteHigh = 1800 × 1.05 × 1.15 = 2173 → 2175
Example 3 — Umbrella, Low risk:
  base = 600, mult = 0.85
  QuoteLow = 600 × 0.85 × 0.90 = 459 → 450
  QuoteHigh = 600 × 0.85 × 1.15 = 587 → 575

# DELIVERY FORMAT (under 25 seconds, after computation)
Sentence 1: "Based on what you've shared, you're looking at roughly $[QuoteLow] to $[QuoteHigh] per year."
Sentence 2: "Just so you know, this is a ballpark — our underwriting team will confirm the final number — but it gives us a good starting point."
Sentence 3: "Want me to walk you through what's included before I put the summary together?"
# IF BUNDLE (BundleFlag = "Yes")
Sentence 1 (instead): "For the full bundle, you're looking at roughly $[QuoteLow] to $[QuoteHigh] per year — that's about [BundleSavingsPercent]% less than buying separately."

# MANDATORY RULES
- QuoteLow and QuoteHigh MUST be positive integers above 0.
- NEVER set them to 0.
- NEVER leave them blank.
- NEVER output text like "TBD" or "approximately 900".
- Output as a plain number string e.g. "925" — no dollar sign, 
  no word "dollars". Both are acceptable to the system.
- ALWAYS say "rough estimate" or "ballpark" in delivery.
- ALWAYS mention "underwriting review" or "underwriting team will confirm" in delivery.
- ALWAYS say a range, never a single number.
# DON'T
- Say "low risk" or "high risk" to the caller.
- Say "Final price" or "your premium will be."
- Skip the cushion sentence.
# EXIT
After delivery and any caller follow-up, transition to N23 (Mid-Call Summary).

variables.name: QuoteLow
variables.description:
The LOW end of the premium range for the caller's selected policy, in U.S. dollars, as a positive integer.
COMPUTATION (executed silently in the prompt):
QuoteLow = base_premium × risk_multiplier × 0.90, rounded to nearest 25.
Where base_premium and risk_multiplier are from the reference tables in N22's prompt.
CRITICAL: This value MUST be a positive integer greater than 0. Never output 0. Never output blank. If unable to compute, use the base premium for SelectedPolicy as fallback (Personal Auto: 1200, Homeowners: 1800, etc.).
Examples:
- Personal Auto + Low risk: 925
- Homeowners + Medium risk: 1700
- Umbrella + Low risk: 450
Output ONLY the integer. No currency symbols ($), no commas, no decimal points, no words. Just the number.

variables.name: QuoteHigh
variables.description:
The HIGH end of the premium range for the caller's selected policy, in U.S. dollars, as a positive integer.
COMPUTATION (executed silently in the prompt):
QuoteHigh = base_premium × risk_multiplier × 1.15, rounded to nearest 25.
Where base_premium and risk_multiplier are from the reference tables in N22's prompt.
CRITICAL: This value MUST be a positive integer greater than QuoteLow. Never output 0. Never output blank. Typical spread: QuoteHigh ≈ QuoteLow × 1.28.
Examples:
- Personal Auto + Low risk: 1175
- Homeowners + Medium risk: 2175
- Umbrella + Low risk: 575
Output ONLY the integer. No currency symbols ($), no commas, no decimal points, no words. Just the number.

variables.name: QuoteFrequency
variables.description:
The time unit for the quote range. Default value for WMIC POC: "annual".
Output exactly one of: "annual" or "monthly".
For POC: always output "annual" unless caller explicitly requests monthly pricing.
Output only the string, no other text.

variables.name: BundleSavingsPercent
variables.description:
The bundle discount percentage to display when delivering the quote. Only relevant when BundleFlag = "Yes".
Logic:
- If BundleFlag = "No": output 0
- If BundleFlag = "Yes": output 10 (POC simplification)
Output only an integer (0 or 10). No percent symbol, no text.

instruction.text:
You are validating the captured information before generating the draft policy summary. This is a mandatory step.

 
Open with:
"Before I put the summary together, let me read back what I have to make sure everything's right."
 
# READ-BACK FORMAT
 
Group by category, keep brief — under 60 seconds total speech.
 
PROFILE:
"You're [age range] in [residence state], [home ownership], [dependents Y/N], [asset bracket]."
 
POLICY DETAILS (per policy):
"For [policy name]: [3-5 most material data points read back]."
 
QUOTE:
"And the estimated range is [LOW] to [HIGH] per year, subject to underwriting review."
 
# CONFIRMATION
 
After read-back, ask:
"Did I get all of that right, or is there anything you'd like to correct?"
 
# IF CORRECTIONS NEEDED
- Acknowledge the correction warmly.
- Update the affected variable.
- Re-confirm just the changed item.
- DO NOT re-read everything.
 
# IF ALL GOOD
"Perfect. Let me put your draft summary together and send it over."
Transition to N24 (Draft Generation).

variables.name: ValidationConfirmed
variables.description:
Capture whether the caller has confirmed that all read-back information is correct, as exactly one of: 'Yes' or 'No'.
- 'Yes' — caller affirmed everything is correct (e.g., "Yes, that's right", "All good", "Correct", "Looks right", "Yeah that's it", silent agreement after read-back, "No corrections needed")
- 'No' — caller indicated at least one item needs correction (e.g., "Wait, my mileage is wrong", "Actually, the address should be...", "Let me correct one thing", "That's not right")
Logic:
- Set to 'Yes' ONLY when the caller has explicitly affirmed accuracy after the read-back AND any corrections they raised have been addressed and re-confirmed
- Set to 'No' if any correction is still pending
- If the caller goes off-topic or asks an unrelated question, do not set the variable yet — wait until they return to confirmation
Note: This variable acts as a compliance gate. The flow should NOT proceed to N24 (Draft Generation) until ValidationConfirmed is 'Yes'.
Output only the exact value 'Yes' or 'No'. Do not output any other text.
